fact_nuggets.text: Bad Dreams (Japanese: Nightmare) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV. It is the signature Ability of Darkrai.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Enables Pokemon to inflict damage on sleeping foes in the same room. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD The Pokemon damages enemy Pokemon in the same room and makes them have bad dreams!

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Pokemon Legends: Z-A 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] At the end of each turn, each adjacent sleeping opponent takes damage equal to 1/8 of its maximum HP. Bad Dreams inflicts damage after a Pokemon that is drowsy due to Yawn falls asleep (and thus inflicts damage to a Pokemon the same turn it falls asleep due to drowsiness). Bad Dreams applies after a Pokemon has the opportunity to be woken up by Hydration , Shed Skin , or Healer . Spoiler warning: this article may contain major plot or ending details. Pokemon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Legends: Z-A , Abilities do not occur in normal gameplay. However, during the Rogue Mega Evolution battle against Darkrai, drowsy Pokemon will take damage equal to 1/25 of their maximum HP every second. When Darkrai Rogue Mega Evolves in the second phase of the fight, the damage increases to 1/16 of maximum HP and occurs more frequently. Darkrai does not retain this effect when caught. Spoilers end here. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Bad Dreams has no effect outside of battle.

Damages sleeping opponents for 1/8 their max HP after each turn.
